21.reason: Error: Using %d prints the integer value of j (e.g., 49 for '1') instead of the character. Should use %c to print the digit character.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 1.0 , reason:  Error: Using %d prints the integer value of j (e.g., 49 for '1') instead of the character. Should use %c to print the digit character.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 1.0 , reason:  Error: Using %d prints the integer value of j (e.g., 49 for '1'), but the expected output is the character itself. Should use %c to print the character.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 1.0 , reason:  Should be printf("%c",j) to print the digit character, not its ASCII value.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 1.0 , reason:  The format specifier should be "%c" instead of "%d". Using "%d" prints the ASCII integer value of the character (e.g., '1' becomes 49), whereas the problem requires printing the character itself.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 1.0 , reason:  The format specifier %d prints the integer ASCII value of the character j, not the character itself. It should be %c to print the digit character.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 1.0 , reason:  [Using %d prints the integer value (ASCII code) instead of the character; should use %c to output digit characters]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 1.0 , reason:  [Using %d prints the integer value (ASCII code) instead of the character; should use %c to output digit characters]
